strengths: 17+ years of continuous engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — meets the 15-year minimum.
Entrepreneurial track record: founded and maintains GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used by real organizations (Green Map), demonstrating independent product ownership.
Active involvement in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling at re:cinq, showing current exposure to agentic AI workflows.
concerns: No advanced degree (Master's/PhD) in an AI-related field; education is two bachelor's degrees in CS and fine arts — a notable gap against preferred qualifications.
AI expertise is shallow and very recent (April 2025 to present only); the resume shows no track record of building or shipping AI-powered products or platforms at scale.

No demonstrated strategic advisory, customer-facing thought leadership, or director-level scope; all roles are hands-on individual contributor with no evidence of influencing AI strategy across organizations.
response.justification: The role requires a director-level AI strategist with recognized, demonstrated expertise in building AI-powered platforms and advising enterprise customers — competencies that are almost entirely absent here. The candidate's AI work is limited to MCP tooling started in April 2025, with no prior AI product history, no publications or talks on AI strategy, and no evidence of strategic customer engagement. Years of experience (17+) and the GISCollective founder story are genuine positives, and the MCP work shows directional curiosity, but the gap between a senior hands-on developer and a Principal AI Strategy IC is too large. The score of 3 reflects the experience-floor being met but the core competencies of the role being unmet.
